description: 'noru' is an open vessel with a rounded or otherwise hollow interior for holding, serving, or eating food. Its greater depth distinguishes it from 'palu' (plate), and it is not made chiefly for heating like 'tomi' (pot). Material and size remain open.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip at the upper teeth for voiced dental nasal 'n' and round the lips into stressed mid-back 'o'. Tap the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for 'r', then raise the back of the tongue and keep the lips rounded for 'u'.

semantic_domains.physical: an open hollow vessel used for food or other contents
